behavioral_patterns.value:
Automated account suspension without human notification or appeal process

Behaviors:
  • Step 1: Implement automated verification requirement with deadline
  • Step 2: Automatically suspend accounts that fail verification
  • Step 3: Block access to publishing systems without human review
  • Step 4: Provide only automated responses to appeals

Detection: Monitor for mass account suspensions followed by spike in automated support responses and lack of human escalation paths
behavioral_patterns.context: Automated account suspension without human notification or appeal process

behavioral_patterns.value:
Supply chain disruption through developer account termination

Behaviors:
  • Step 1: Target developer accounts for critical software projects
  • Step 2: Suspend accounts without warning during active development cycles
  • Step 3: Block ability to publish security updates and patches
  • Step 4: Force users to rely on potentially outdated or vulnerable software

Detection: Monitor for sudden cessation of software updates from multiple high-profile projects simultaneously, especially security-critical tools
behavioral_patterns.context: Supply chain disruption through developer account termination

behavioral_patterns.value:
Communication breakdown leading to service disruption

Behaviors:
  • Step 1: Send notifications through channels that may not reach recipients
  • Step 2: Implement automated enforcement without human oversight
  • Step 3: Route all support requests to automated systems
  • Step 4: Require public pressure or media attention to trigger human response

Detection: Track patterns of service disruptions that only get resolved after public social media campaigns or media coverage
behavioral_patterns.context: Communication breakdown leading to service disruption

behavioral_patterns.value:
Critical security update blockage through platform control

Behaviors:
  • Step 1: Control mandatory signing/publishing infrastructure
  • Step 2: Suspend developer access during verification process
  • Step 3: Block emergency security patch deployment
  • Step 4: Create extended appeal process during active security threats

Detection: Monitor for delays in critical security updates coinciding with developer platform access issues, especially for widely-used security tools
behavioral_patterns.context: Critical security update blockage through platform control

behavioral_patterns.value:
Escalation through public pressure and media attention

Behaviors:
  • Step 1: Developers exhaust normal support channels without resolution
  • Step 2: Public disclosure of service disruption on social media
  • Step 3: Media coverage amplifies the issue
  • Step 4: Executive intervention triggered by public attention
  • Step 5: Rapid resolution after public pressure

Detection: Track correlation between public complaints about platform issues and subsequent rapid executive responses, indicating broken normal escalation paths
behavioral_patterns.context: Escalation through public pressure and media attention

snowflake_sql:
-- KTLYST Detection Rules
-- Generated from 1 rules
-- ============================================

-- Rule 1: Detect Behavior: especially security-critical tools
-- ID: IOC-BEHAVIOR-0362d2b1
-- Generation: template (success)
-- Description: Detects behavior indicator 'especially security-critical tools' extracted from threat intelligence.
-- ------------------------------------------------------------
-- Behavioral Pattern Detection: especially security-critical tools
-- Source: behavioral_1
-- TTP: T1195
-- Kill Chain: 
-- Type: unknown

SELECT
    case_id,
    user_id,
    case_notes,
    case_description,
    created_at,
    'especially security-critical tools' AS matched_pattern,
    'T1195' AS ttp_code,
    'behavioral_1' AS source_span
FROM case_notes
WHERE (
    REGEXP_LIKE(LOWER(case_notes), LOWER('\\bespecially\ security\-critical\ tools\\b'))
    OR REGEXP_LIKE(LOWER(case_description), LOWER('\\bespecially\ security\-critical\ tools\\b'))
)
AND created_at >= DATEADD(day, -90, CURRENT_TIMESTAMP())
ORDER BY created_at DESC;

compiled_outputs.sentinel:
// KTLYST Detection Rules (KQL for Microsoft Sentinel)
// Generated: 5 rules from 5 indicators
// ============================================================

// Rule 1: Behavioral Detection - Automated account suspension without human notification or a
// Category: credential_access
// Source: Page 1
// MITRE: 
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(24h)
| where ResultType != "0"
| summarize FailedCount = count(),
    UniqueIPs = dcount(IPAddress),
    UniqueLocations = dcount(Location),
    FirstAttempt = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastAttempt = max(TimeGenerated)
    by UserPrincipalName
| where FailedCount >= 5 or UniqueIPs >= 3
| sort by FailedCount desc
| take 100

// Rule 2: Behavioral Detection - Supply chain disruption through developer account terminatio
// Category: supply_chain
// Source: Page 1
// MITRE: T1195
DeviceProcessEvents
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(24h)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("npm.exe", "pip.exe", "pip3.exe", "nuget.exe")
    or ProcessCommandLine has_any ("install", "update", "upgrade")
| summarize InstallCount = count(),
    UniquePackages = dcount(ProcessCommandLine),
    FirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastSeen = max(TimeGenerated)
    by DeviceName, InitiatingProcessAccountName
| where InstallCount >= 5
| sort by InstallCount desc
| take 100

// Rule 3: Behavioral Detection - Communication breakdown leading to service disruption
// Category: credential_access
// Source: Page 1
// MITRE: 
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(24h)
| where ResultType != "0"
| summarize FailedCount = count(),
    UniqueIPs = dcount(IPAddress),
    UniqueLocations = dcount(Location),
    FirstAttempt = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastAttempt = max(TimeGenerated)
    by UserPrincipalName
| where FailedCount >= 5 or UniqueIPs >= 3
| sort by FailedCount desc
| take 100

// Rule 4: Behavioral Detection - Critical security update blockage through platform control
// Category: credential_access
// Source: Page 1
// MITRE: 
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(24h)
| where ResultType != "0"
| summarize FailedCount = count(),
    UniqueIPs = dcount(IPAddress),
    UniqueLocations = dcount(Location),
    FirstAttempt = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastAttempt = max(TimeGenerated)
    by UserPrincipalName
| where FailedCount >= 5 or UniqueIPs >= 3
| sort by FailedCount desc
| take 100

// Rule 5: Behavioral Detection - Escalation through public pressure and media attention
// Category: credential_access
// Source: Page 1
// MITRE: 
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(24h)
| where ResultType != "0"
| summarize FailedCount = count(),
    UniqueIPs = dcount(IPAddress),
    UniqueLocations = dcount(Location),
    FirstAttempt = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastAttempt = max(TimeGenerated)
    by UserPrincipalName
| where FailedCount >= 5 or UniqueIPs >= 3
| sort by FailedCount desc
| take 100
compiled_outputs.splunk:
``` KTLYST Detection Rules (SPL for Splunk)
``` Generated: 5 rules from 5 indicators

``` Rule 1: Behavioral Detection - Automated account suspension without human notification or a
``` Category: credential_access
``` Source: Page 1
``` MITRE: 
index=main sourcetype=authentication action=failure
| stats count as failed_count, dc(src_ip) as unique_ips, earliest(_time) as first_attempt, latest(_time) as last_attempt by user
| where failed_count >= 5 OR unique_ips >= 3
| sort -failed_count
| table user, failed_count, unique_ips, first_attempt, last_attempt

``` Rule 2: Behavioral Detection - Supply chain disruption through developer account terminatio
``` Category: supply_chain
``` Source: Page 1
``` MITRE: T1195
index=main sourcetype=endpoint:process process_name IN ("npm.exe", "pip.exe", "pip3.exe", "nuget.exe") (process_command_line="*install*" OR process_command_line="*update*")
| stats count as install_count, dc(process_command_line) as unique_packages, earliest(_time) as first_seen, latest(_time) as last_seen by dest, user
| where install_count >= 5
| sort -install_count
| table dest, user, install_count, unique_packages, first_seen, last_seen

``` Rule 3: Behavioral Detection - Communication breakdown leading to service disruption
``` Category: credential_access
``` Source: Page 1
``` MITRE: 
index=main sourcetype=authentication action=failure

``` MITRE: 
index=main sourcetype=authentication action=failure

``` Rule 5: Behavioral Detection - Escalation through public pressure and media attention

``` Source: Page 1
``` MITRE: 
index=main sourcetype=authentication action=failure
| stats count as failed_count, dc(src_ip) as unique_ips, earliest(_time) as first_attempt, latest(_time) as last_attempt by user
| where failed_count >= 5 OR unique_ips >= 3
| sort -failed_count
| table user, failed_count, unique_ips, first_attempt, last_attempt

# KTLYST Detection Rules (SIGMA format)
# Generated: 5 rules from 5 indicators
# Convert to platform-native with: sigma convert -t <target>
---

title: 'KTLYST - Automated account suspension without human notification or a'
id: 6bef3072-2363-545e-85e7-ba8474ec308b
status: experimental
description: |
    Auto-generated detection rule.
    Source provenance: Page 1
level: medium
author: KTLYST
date: 2026/03/24
references:
    - 'KTLYST source: Page 1'
tags:
    - attack.detection
logsource:
    category: authentication
    product: any
detection:
    selection:
        status: 'failure'
    filter_low_count:
        count|gte: 5
    condition: selection
falsepositives:
    - Legitimate administrative activity

---

title: 'KTLYST - Supply chain disruption through developer account terminatio'

    condition: selection_process and selection_action
